purpose: Wizard surface shown by GitManagerView when the active repo's folder exists but lacks a .git directory (api.gitIsRepo returned false). Renders a centered card with a 'Not a Git Repository' title and an 'Initialize Repository' primary button. On click, calls api.gitInit(repoPath) and on success transitions through three visual states: initializing → success (CheckIcon + 'Repository Initialized') → onInitComplete callback (delayed 800ms so the user sees the success state). On failure, displays the error inline via git-manager-error class.

exports.name: InitFlow
exports.signature: { repoPath: string, repoName: string, onInitComplete?: () => void }

gotchas: onInitComplete fires after a 800ms setTimeout to let the user see the success state — but the timeout is not cleared on unmount, so if the component unmounts during the delay the callback may fire on an unmounted parent (low-impact but a React-warning candidate).
api.gitInit is the only required IPC method — if missing, the button does nothing silently.
There is no retry mechanism on error — the user can click again because initializing resets to false in finally, but error state persists until the next click.
After success the parent (GitManagerView.handleInitComplete) calls setIsGitRepo(true) which triggers the data-load useEffect — InitFlow itself doesn't load any git data.
patterns: Three-state UI: idle/initializing/success — driven by initializing and success flags.
Reuses git-manager-empty container class for the centered card — same style as GitWelcome.
conventions: Inline SVG icons (GitInitIcon, CheckIcon, SpinnerIcon) at top of file.
Error display via git-manager-error class with git-manager-error-message child.
